source_pr.title: planner/infoschema: add predicate pushdown for system tables
source_pr.body: ### What problem does this PR solve? Issue Number: ref #50305 Problem Summary: See issue. ### What changed and how does it work? 1. collect predicates on infoschema.tables(statistics,KEY_COLUMN_USAGE,REFERENTIAL_CONSTRAINTS) 2. filter when doing memtable retrieve 3. do column pruning when read infoschema.tables to avoid updating statscache. ### Check List Tests - [x] Unit test - [x] Integration test - [x] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below) - [ ] No need to test > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed. > In cluster with 17w+ tables Side effects - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU - [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory - [ ] Breaking backward compatibility Documentation - [ ] Affects user behaviors - [ ] Contains syntax changes - [ ] Contains variable changes - [ ] Contains experimental features - [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility ### Release note Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note. ```release-note None ```

symptoms: fix flaky test | desc format='brief' SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.statistics WHERE index_name = 'idx1' AND table_schema = DATABASE () AND table_name = 't' ORDER BY seq_in_index; (tests/integrationtest/r/executor/explainfor.result)
failure_signature: fix flaky test | desc format='brief' SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.statistics WHERE index_name = 'idx1' AND table_schema = DATABASE () AND table_name = 't' ORDER BY seq_in_index; (tests/integrationtest/r/executor/explainfor.result)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.
fix_pattern: Sort results / enforce deterministic ordering before assertions to avoid nondeterministic flakiness.

evidence: evidence: fix flaky test | desc format='brief' SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.statistics WHERE index_name = 'idx1' AND table_schema = DATABASE () AND table_name = 't' ORDER BY seq_in_index; (tests/integrationtest/r/executor/explainfor.result)
